articles.title: US-Iran talks open in Switzerland under Qatari-Pakistani mediation; Vance touts 'great progress'
articles.summary: Technical-level US-Iran negotiations opened Sunday at the Burgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne, launching a 60-day window under the 14-point Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding. Iran's delegation was led by parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf with FM Abbas Araghchi; the US side included VP JD Vance, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with Pakistan's PM Shehbaz Sharif and Qatar's PM Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani mediating. The first round ran about 80 minutes and covered frozen assets, sanctions relief and the nuclear file; Vance said the sides 'already made great progress'.

articles.title: Iran delegation walks out after Trump threatens to 'hit Iran very hard again' over Hezbollah
articles.summary: As the Switzerland talks began, President Trump posted on Truth Social demanding Iran 'immediately stop their highly paid PROXIES in Lebanon' or the US would 'hit Iran very hard again, just like we did last week, only harder'. Iran's state agency IRNA said its delegation left the negotiation building in protest after meeting Qatari mediators; a senior US diplomat denied the team had departed, saying negotiations were ongoing. Chief negotiator Ghalibaf responded: 'No matter what they say, we are the ones who act.'

articles.title: Saudi, Egyptian, Pakistani and Turkish FMs back US-Iran talks at Cairo meeting
articles.summary: The foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Pakistan and Türkiye met in Cairo on Sunday and pressed for swift progress in the US-Iran negotiations, signalling broad regional support for a diplomatic path to end the war and stabilise Lebanon.

articles.title: Israel orders troops to hold positions in Lebanon, halt fire except at Ali al-Taher Hill; soldier killed
articles.summary: Netanyahu and Defence Minister Israel Katz ordered the IDF to remain in occupied Lebanese territory and to hold fire everywhere except Ali al-Taher Hill, the focus of repeated clashes with Hezbollah. The army announced Staff Sgt. Liav Kababia, 20, was killed in Lebanon, underscoring the fragility of the renewed ceasefire.

articles.title: At least 29 killed in Israeli strikes across south Lebanon a day after renewed ceasefire
articles.summary: At least 29 people were killed when Israeli forces struck across southern Lebanon a day after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a renewed ceasefire. Iran warned it would not enter a broader agreement with Washington unless Israel halts its attacks on Lebanon, the central sticking point hanging over the Switzerland talks.

articles.title: Conflicting claims over Strait of Hormuz as Iran says it closed waterway, US disputes
articles.summary: Iran's military command said it had again closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israel's strikes in Lebanon, calling them a breach of the deal with Washington. US Central Command countered that commercial traffic actually rose, with 55 merchant ships and more than 17 million barrels of oil transiting, and that 'Iran does not control the Strait'. Trump vowed Iran would not levy transit tolls, though he suggested the US might.

articles.title: IDF says it captured a Hezbollah drone factory hidden inside a south Lebanon mountain
articles.summary: The Israeli military said it seized a Hezbollah drone manufacturing facility and launch site concealed inside a mountain in southern Lebanon, part of operations it says are aimed at degrading the group's fibre-optic drone capability.

articles.title: Netanyahu may move to dissolve Knesset within days amid Haredi draft standoff
articles.summary: Thwarted by ultra-Orthodox intransigence over a military conscription bill, Netanyahu is reported to be weighing dissolving the Knesset within days, raising the prospect of early elections as the US-Iran diplomacy sidelines Israel.
